item.title: The Bizarre Story Of Lisa Nowak
item.description: NASA astronaut Lisa Nowak drove nearly 900 miles from Houston to Orlando in February 2007 to confront Colleen Shipman, a woman romantically involved with a fellow astronaut. She pleaded guilty to felony burglary and misdemeanor battery in 2009 and received one year of probation. NASA terminated her and the Navy discharged her under other than honorable conditions.

media:content.media:title: The Bizarre Story Of Lisa Nowak
media:content.media:description: NASA astronaut Lisa Nowak drove nearly 900 miles from Houston to Orlando in February 2007 to confront Colleen Shipman, a woman romantically involved with a fellow astronaut. She pleaded guilty to felony burglary and misdemeanor battery in 2009 and received one year of probation. NASA terminated her and the Navy discharged her under other than honorable conditions.

item.title: This IRS Agent's Affair With His Au Pair Turned Into a Murder Plot
item.description:
Brendan Banfield, a Virginia resident, was charged in connection with a 2023 case involving the deaths of his wife and another man at their home. Authorities said the incident followed an online catfishing scheme involving a third individual.
Investigators alleged Banfield helped create false online identities that led another person to the home. That individual was later involved in the fatal incident, which also resulted in his own death.
Banfield was convicted on multiple charges related to the case. The investigation highlighted how online deception played a role in the events leading to the deaths.

media:content.media:title: This IRS Agent's Affair With His Au Pair Turned Into a Murder Plot
media:content.media:description:
Brendan Banfield, a Virginia resident, was charged in connection with a 2023 case involving the deaths of his wife and another man at their home. Authorities said the incident followed an online catfishing scheme involving a third individual.
Investigators alleged Banfield helped create false online identities that led another person to the home. That individual was later involved in the fatal incident, which also resulted in his own death.
Banfield was convicted on multiple charges related to the case. The investigation highlighted how online deception played a role in the events leading to the deaths.

item.title: I Took My Daughters’ Lives to Prevent a Darker Fate!
item.description:
Monica McCarrick was convicted in California for the 2010 deaths of her three year old twin daughters, Tori and Lily Ball, in Fairfield. Firefighters discovered the children after responding to a fire at the apartment.
A jury later found McCarrick guilty of two counts of first degree murder and assault on a child causing death. She had pleaded not guilty and not guilty by reason of insanity.
She was sentenced to two consecutive life terms in prison without the possibility of parole following the verdict.

media:content.media:title: I Took My Daughters’ Lives to Prevent a Darker Fate!
media:content.media:description:
Monica McCarrick was convicted in California for the 2010 deaths of her three year old twin daughters, Tori and Lily Ball, in Fairfield. Firefighters discovered the children after responding to a fire at the apartment.
A jury later found McCarrick guilty of two counts of first degree murder and assault on a child causing death. She had pleaded not guilty and not guilty by reason of insanity.
She was sentenced to two consecutive life terms in prison without the possibility of parole following the verdict.

item.title: The Terrifying Story Of Richard Speck
item.description: Richard Speck broke into a Chicago townhouse on July 13, 1966, and killed eight student nurses over several hours. One survivor, Corazon Amurao, hid under a bed and later identified him in court. Speck was convicted on all eight counts in 1967 and died in prison of a heart attack in 1991.

media:content.media:title: The Terrifying Story Of Richard Speck
media:content.media:description: Richard Speck broke into a Chicago townhouse on July 13, 1966, and killed eight student nurses over several hours. One survivor, Corazon Amurao, hid under a bed and later identified him in court. Speck was convicted on all eight counts in 1967 and died in prison of a heart attack in 1991.
